description_long: A cluster of tall, slender bamboo stalks rises from a shared base, each stem supporting a delicate spray of narrow, elongated green leaves. The bamboo canes are thin and straight, with a subtle yellow-green hue and visible nodes along their length. The foliage is airy and feathery, creating a light, natural canopy at the top of the stems. The plant appears to be arranged in a loose, organic grouping, with some stalks leaning gently outward. There is no visible pot or container, suggesting the base is either hidden or the plant is intended to be placed directly into a planter or the ground. The overall appearance is elegant and natural, making it suitable for decorative use in urban parks, sidewalks, or modern outdoor spaces.
description: A cluster of tall, slender bamboo stalks with feathery green leaves, arranged in a natural, airy grouping. The base is not visibly potted, and the plant exudes a light, elegant presence.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cluster of bamboo stalks and dense, feathery foliage at the top.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thinness of the stalks and the outward lean of the bamboo stems.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the vertical alignment of the stalks and the sparse, airy leaf arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the arching lean of the bamboo stems and the delicate spread of leaves.
